title: carex scitula
intro: Carex scitula, also known as the rough sedge, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sedge family. It is native to North America and has been used by indigenous communities for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and its health benefits also include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health. In herbal preparations, Carex scitula is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also used in traditional medicine to make poultices and salves for topical application.
